disclosure_items.clinical_fact: Postprandial fullness and early satiety for about four weeks, with a severe constant epigastric burning over the last 48 hours
trigger_question_examples: When did this start and how has it changed?
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: For about a month I have felt full very quickly after eating, and for the last two days there has been a severe burning in the top of my stomach that just won't stop.

disclosure_items.clinical_fact: Takes daily ibuprofen for chronic knee pain (stopped 48 hours ago because of the stomach pain)
trigger_question_examples: Do you take any regular painkillers or anti-inflammatories?
Are you on anything for your knee?
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: Yes — I take ibuprofen every day for my knee, though I stopped it two days ago when my stomach got bad.

disclosure_items.clinical_fact: No blood in vomit and no black or tarry stools
trigger_question_examples: Have you vomited any blood or anything like coffee grounds?
Have your stools been black or tarry?
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: No, I haven't been sick with any blood and my stools have looked normal — no black colour.

disclosure_items.clinical_fact: No difficulty swallowing and no unintentional weight loss
trigger_question_examples: Any trouble swallowing food or drink?
Have you lost weight without trying?
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: No trouble swallowing, and I haven't lost any weight that I know of — this has only been going a few weeks.

disclosure_items.clinical_fact: Pain stays in the upper middle abdomen and does not spread to the jaw, neck or arm; no cold sweat or breathlessness
trigger_question_examples: Does the pain spread to your chest, jaw or arm?
Any cold sweat, breathlessness or feeling it in your chest?
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: No, it stays in my stomach — it doesn't go up into my chest or arm, and I'm not sweaty or short of breath.

disclosure_items.clinical_fact: Reduced food and fluid intake for 48 hours with weakness; the over-the-counter antacids have not helped
trigger_question_examples: How much have you been able to eat and drink?
Have the antacids helped at all?
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: I've barely eaten or drunk anything for two days because I fill up and it burns, and I feel really weak. The antacids haven't done anything.

disclosure_items.clinical_fact: Associated mild nausea, frequent belching and upper-abdominal bloating; no relief with opening the bowels
trigger_question_examples: Any other symptoms — nausea, burping, bloating?
Does going to the toilet change the pain?
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: I feel a little sick, I'm burping a lot and my upper belly feels bloated. Going to the toilet doesn't make any difference.
